meta.description: Edge case inputs for Client Remy — tests graceful handling of unusual, malformed, or boundary inputs

prompts.message: Can I book a dinner for next Saturday? 🎉🍷🥩
我想预订一个周六晚餐
prompts.description: Chinese — should get a response (likely in English)

prompts.message: Quiero reservar una cena para 8 personas el sábado
مرحبا، أريد حجز عشاء
a
hi
prompts.description: Lots of whitespace around a word

prompts.message: WHAT IS THE STATUS OF MY EVENT AND WHEN IS IT AND HOW MANY GUESTS AND WHAT IS THE MENU AND HOW MUCH DOES IT COST AND WHEN DO I PAY AND WHAT TIME DOES THE CHEF ARRIVE AND DOES THE CHEF CLEAN UP
prompts.description: ALL CAPS shouting — should still answer calmly

prompts.message: hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi hi
What's the status of my event? (the one on 3/15) — I need an update ASAP!!!
I need dinner for 10 people @ $100/person or less — is that doable?? Let me know ASAP & send the quote to me@email.com
Can you make the menu bold?
prompts.description: HTML/XSS injection attempt — should be treated as text

prompts.message: SELECT * FROM events WHERE client_id = '1'; DROP TABLE events;--
prompts.description: SQL injection attempt — should be treated as text

prompts.message: yes
prompts.description: Context-free affirmative — Remy should ask what they mean

prompts.message: no
thanks
prompts.description: Just a thank you — Remy should respond warmly

prompts.message: nevermind
hello
prompts.description: Simple greeting — should get a warm welcome

prompts.message: So here's the thing, I've been going back and forth about this dinner party for weeks now and I keep changing my mind about the menu and the guest count and the date and honestly I'm not even sure if I want to do a formal sit-down dinner or more of a casual cocktail party with passed appetizers and small bites because the last time we did a formal dinner it was kind of stiff and nobody really relaxed until dessert was served and by then people were starting to leave so maybe something more relaxed would be better but then again my mother-in-law is coming and she expects a proper dinner with courses and wine pairings and she'll judge me if we just have appetizers standing up in the living room so I don't know what to do can you help me figure this out because I'm stressed about it and I just want everyone to have a good time and the food to be amazing and not too expensive
prompts.description: Long stream-of-consciousness message — Remy should extract the core question

prompts.message: I want a vegan dinner but also a big steak for the main course
I need dinner for 0 people
prompts.description: Zero guest count — should ask for clarification

prompts.message: I want to book dinner for yesterday
prompts.description: Past date booking — should note the date issue

prompts.message: Are you a real person or a bot?
prompts.description: Identity question — should acknowledge being AI concierge without robotic language
